factors.name: USEP wholesale trend
factors.note: USEP has been trending upward over 3 observed days, suggesting higher fuel costs will feed into the tariff.

factors.name: Carbon tax
factors.note: Carbon tax at S$45/tCO2e (2026-2027). Already priced into current tariff — no additional impact this quarter.

factors.name: Geopolitical: Hormuz blockade
factors.note: The Strait of Hormuz blockade (from Feb 2026) has pushed up global LNG and oil prices. Fuel costs rose sharply in March-May 2026, which will feed into the Q3 tariff review.

factors.name: Fuel cost pass-through lag
factors.note: The energy cost component uses a ~2.5-month lagged fuel price window. Recent fuel price moves take 1-2 quarters to fully reflect in the tariff.

prediction.reasoning: Upward pressure from: USEP wholesale trend, Geopolitical: Hormuz blockade. The tariff is likely to rise.

recommendation: Lock in a fixed-rate plan now before the tariff and retail prices rise. Current FR plans are already below the regulated tariff — this advantage will shrink.
disclaimer: This is a directional estimate based on public wholesale electricity price patterns. The actual regulated tariff is set quarterly by SP Group under EMA guidelines and depends on natural gas prices, carbon tax, and grid costs.
